target.title: test(acpx): accept built-dist MCP server resolution when dist exists
target.body:
test(acpx): mirror resolver's path construction in MCP server test helper

The ACPx config test expected source-mode (tsx loader) paths for built-in MCP servers, but the resolver correctly prefers dist entries when they exist. Update the test helper to match this runtime behavior.

- Mirror resolver's path construction: compute openClawRoot from resolveAcpxPluginRoot, then use path.join/path.relative to build the correct absolute dist path.
- Remove the broken entrypoint.replace("/src/", "/dist/") which never matched because the test passes "src/mcp/..." without a leading separator.

Fixes #80431

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The test helper `expectedMcpServerArgs` used `entrypoint.replace("/src/", "/dist/")` which never matched because the caller passes "src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.ts" (no leading `/`). This caused the dist-existence check to silently check the wrong path (`src/mcp/*.js` instead of `dist/mcp/*.js`) and always fall back to source-mode args, even when `dist/` existed.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu 22.04, Node 20, pnpm 9. Built dist exists for built-in MCP servers under `dist/mcp/`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
cd /tmp/openclaw-fork
git checkout fix/issue-80431-acpx-dist-path
pnpm build
ls -la dist/mcp/  # confirm dist exists
npx vitest run extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**
Terminal output showing the corrected test helper now constructs the correct absolute dist path:
```
$ npx vitest run extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
 PASS  extensions/acpx/src/config.test.ts
   ✓ injects the built-in plugin-tools MCP server only when explicitly enabled
   ✓ injects the built-in openclaw-tools MCP server only when explicitly enabled
```

Before the fix, the helper computed `distEntry` as `src/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js` (because `/src/` did not match) and `fs.existsSync` returned false, so the test always fell back to source-mode args even when `dist/mcp/plugin-tools-serve.js` existed.

**Observed result after fix:** The helper now computes the same absolute dist path as the production resolver (`resolvePluginToolsMcpServerConfig`), correctly returning `[distEntry]` when `dist/` is present and falling back to `["--import", tsx, sourceEntry]` otherwise.

**What was not tested:** No runtime resolver logic changed; only the test helper was updated to mirror the resolver's path construction.
target.author: khancold
